glioma_tumor: MRI image showing a glioma tumor, a malignant brain tumor arising from glial cells.
Glioma tumors often appear as irregular, infiltrative masses with variable contrast enhancement.
The image may reveal surrounding edema, mass effect, and heterogeneous signal intensity.
Gliomas can cross the midline and invade adjacent brain structures, making them aggressive in nature.
This class is characterized by poorly defined borders and possible necrotic or cystic regions within the tumor.
meningioma_tumor: MRI image showing a glioma tumor, a malignant brain tumor arising from glial cells.
Glioma tumors often appear as irregular, infiltrative masses with variable contrast enhancement.
The image may reveal surrounding edema, mass effect, and heterogeneous signal intensity.
Gliomas can cross the midline and invade adjacent brain structures, making them aggressive in nature.
This class is characterized by poorly defined borders and possible necrotic or cystic regions within the tumor.
normal_brain: MRI image showing a meningioma tumor, a typically benign tumor arising from the meninges of the brain.
Meningiomas often appear as well-circumscribed, extra-axial masses with strong, homogeneous contrast enhancement.
The image may show a dural tail sign and displacement of adjacent brain tissue without invasion.
Meningiomas are usually slow-growing and may cause compression of nearby structures.
This class is characterized by a clear boundary between the tumor and normal brain parenchyma.
pituitary_tumor: MRI image showing a pituitary tumor, a mass arising from the pituitary gland at the base of the brain.
Pituitary tumors often appear as well-defined, sellar or suprasellar masses with variable enhancement.
The image may reveal compression of the optic chiasm or extension into surrounding structures.
Pituitary tumors can be functional or non-functional, affecting hormone production.
This class is characterized by a distinct mass in the region of the sella turcica, separate from other brain tumors.
